Check.definition: a written order directing a bank to pay money

Therapist.definition: a person skilled in a particular type of therapy

Massage.definition: kneading and rubbing parts of the body to increase circulation and promote relaxation

Pressure.definition: the force applied to a unit area of surface; measured in pascals (SI unit) or in dynes (cgs unit)

Examination.definition: the act of examining something closely (as for mistakes)

Exam.definition: a set of questions or exercises evaluating skill or knowledge

Press.definition: the act of pressing; the exertion of pressure
exert pressure or force to or upon

Therapy.definition: (medicine) the act of caring for someone (as by medication or remedial training etc.)

Nurse.definition: one skilled in caring for young children or the sick (usually under the supervision of a physician)

Test.definition: trying something to find out about it
a set of questions or exercises evaluating skill or knowledge

Treat.definition: subject to a process or treatment, with the aim of readying for some purpose, improving, or remedying a condition

Treatment.definition: care provided to improve a situation (especially medical procedures or applications that are intended to relieve illness or injury)

Hospital.definition: a health facility where patients receive treatment

Medical.definition: a thorough physical examination; includes a variety of tests depending on the age and sex and health of the person

Injection.definition: the forceful insertion of a substance under pressure
the act of putting a liquid into the body by means of a syringe

Surgery.definition: the branch of medical science that treats disease or injury by operative procedures

Operation.definition: a medical procedure involving an incision with instruments; performed to repair damage or arrest disease in a living body

Tender.definition: make tender or more tender as by marinating, pounding, or applying a tenderizer

Medication.definition: (medicine) something that treats or prevents or alleviates the symptoms of disease

Pills.definition: something that resembles a tablet of medicine in shape or size
a dose of medicine in the form of a small pellet

Ointment.definition: semisolid preparation (usually containing a medicine) applied externally as a remedy or for soothing an irritation
